issues.issue: Uniform scene length and choice cadence - every branch scene is roughly the same short length (4-5 lines), and choices land at almost perfectly even intervals (every ~2 scenes)
issues.impact: Creates a metronomic, slightly mechanical rhythm; player can predict 'choice is coming' which reduces surprise and tension variation
issues.fix: Vary scene length deliberately - let the hallway mockery scene breathe longer with more beats before the choice, and let a couple of aftermath scenes be very short (2 lines) to create rhythmic contrast

issues.issue: Choice 5 (presentation authentic vs safe) only has 2 options instead of the 3-option pattern established elsewhere
issues.impact: Breaks established choice-format consistency; also removes a 'middle path' or clever third option that other choices offer, making this decision feel more binary/less expressive
issues.fix: Add a third option, e.g. a compromise where player suggests blending the poster format with one illustrated panel - giving an ADAPTABILITY-flavored middle path

issues.issue: Trait mapping is often redundant with narrative outcome - the 'right' emotional choice (defend Priya, sit with Priya, authentic idea) is also clearly telegraphed as the 'better' choice by narrative tone
issues.impact: Reduces genuine dilemma quality; experienced players will quickly learn to always pick empathy options, making replay for 'different values' less interesting since one path is narratively rewarded
issues.fix: Give the popularity/self-protective paths their own distinct narrative payoff (e.g., Jordan's path unlocks a scene where the player genuinely helps Jordan with his insecurity) so it's not just 'the worse choice'

issues.issue: Player has zero input scenes between major choices - the connective tissue scenes (scene-project-assigned, scene-presentation-day) are all narrator/NPC-driven with the player as passive recipient
issues.impact: Player periodically becomes a spectator; two 'no-choice' bridge scenes back to back around the project section slow agency momentum
issues.fix: Add a small optional reactive beat/dialogue flavor line for player even in bridge scenes, or trim scene-project-assigned into the following choice scene directly

issues.issue: Only 3 named 'outcomes/paths' are documented despite 3^5 possible combinatorial branches; most combinations funnel into same 3 branchDialogue variants
issues.impact: Mixed-trait playthroughs (e.g., confident+empathetic but neutral in hallway) may not get a distinctly tailored ending flavor beyond the three broad archetypes
issues.fix: Add a few more mixed-branch dialogue variants (e.g., a 'complicated' branch for players with 1-2 genuine choices but not full) to reward more varied builds

issues.issue: Opening scene-arrival is very short and low-stakes with no choice at all, delaying first interactive moment until scene-homeroom
issues.impact: First 60-90 seconds is pure passive reading before first choice; risk of losing low-patience players in first minute
issues.fix: Consider a tiny micro-choice in the arrival scene itself (e.g., how the player physically enters - confidently vs cautiously) even if low-stakes, to get hands on controls immediately

strengths: Excellent central choice (hallway mockery) - genuinely tense, clearly framed, real stakes, strong emotional payoff regardless of choice
Trait mapping is thoughtful and consistent, reinforcing CONFIDENCE/EMPATHY/ADAPTABILITY meaningfully across all 6 choices
Distinct character voice maintained across branches (Jordan's bravado, Priya's guardedness) making replays feel textually fresh even in repeated locations
Branching endings with branchDialogue variants that cross-reference prior choices (e.g., popularity ending acknowledging genuine connection choice) add nice narrative memory
Clear three-path archetype design (Genuine/Popularity/Self-Protective) gives strong replay hooks with thematically opposed philosophies
Good use of INTERNAL dialogue for tutorializing emotional stakes without breaking immersion
recommendations: Vary scene lengths more deliberately to create pacing rhythm rather than uniform beats
Add a third 'middle path' option to choice-5 for consistency and expressive range
Build a redemption/payoff scene for the popularity/self-protective paths so they don't read as simply 'the worse choice'
Add micro-choice or reactive prompt in opening scene to hook player agency immediately
Consider surfacing a trait meter or subtle indicator so players can see impact of choices building over the episode (supports agency/feedback loop)

summary.verdict: A well-constructed, emotionally coherent episode with a genuinely strong central moral choice (hallway mockery) that anchors the whole experience. Pacing is slightly too uniform and some choices skew toward an implied 'correct' answer, but the trait system, character writing, and branch design deliver a solid, replayable player experience appropriate for its target age group.
keyIssues: Uniform scene rhythm reduces tension variation
Choice 5 breaks 3-option pattern and offers less expressive range
Popularity/self-protective paths read as narratively 'punished' rather than equally valid playstyles
topStrengths: Hallway mockery scene is a standout, well-designed moral choice
Strong consistent character voices across all branches
Cross-referencing branch dialogue rewards attentive replay
mustFix: Give popularity/neutral paths their own narrative validation, not just 'lesser' outcomes
niceToHave: Vary scene lengths for pacing rhythm
Add trait-impact feedback visibility for player
